requirements_addressed: Output directory and metadata schema bound at factory time
Append mode with single JSON lines
Success message format with example numbering
Embedded newlines handled as single JSON line
Consistent state on partial write failure
D7: all errors as strings, never exceptions

completion_promises.evidence: create_write_output_tool returns a LangChain StructuredTool (BaseTool subclass) decorated with @tool. Tests verify isinstance(write_tool, BaseTool), tool.name == 'write_output', and tool.description is non-empty.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Output directory and metadata schema are bound at factory time
completion_promises.evidence: Factory accepts output_dir: Path and metadata_schema: list[MetadataField] as parameters. These are captured in the closure and used by the inner write_output function. Tests verify different output_dirs produce files in different directories and different schemas validate differently (strict rejects 'hamlet', loose accepts it).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Validation checks executed in order per API contract (10 checks)
completion_promises.evidence: All 10 validation steps implemented in sequence: (1) JSON parse, (2) messages exists/non-empty array, (3) messages[0].role == 'system', (4) metadata exists/is object, (5) layer in {behaviour, knowledge}, (6) type in {reasoning, direct}, (7) reasoning requires <think>, (8) direct rejects <think>, (9) schema valid_values check, (10) append to file. TestValidationChain class has 15 tests covering each step. TestValidationOrder class verifies ordering (step 1 before 2, step 2 before 4, step 3 before 5, step 5 before 6).

Layer routing implemented via _LAYER_PATHS dict mapping 'behaviour' to 'train.jsonl' and 'knowledge' to 'rag_index/knowledge.jsonl'. TestLayerRouting verifies behaviour creates train.jsonl (not knowledge.jsonl), knowledge creates rag_index/knowledge.jsonl (not train.jsonl), and rag_index/ directory is auto-created.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Append mode - each example appended as single JSON line
completion_promises.evidence: File opened with mode 'a' (append). Each example serialized with json.dumps and written as line + '\n'. TestAppendMode verifies two writes produce two lines, and each line is valid JSON with messages and metadata keys.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Atomic writes - each line flushed immediately (no buffering)
completion_promises.evidence: f.flush() called immediately after f.write(line + '\n'). This ensures data is pushed to the OS buffer after each example.

Success message uses f-string: f'Written to {target_path} (example #{count})'. Per-file counters maintained in closure dict example_counts. TestSuccessMessage verifies first example is #1, second is #2, and knowledge layer has independent counter.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Error messages match API contract exactly
completion_promises.evidence: All error messages match API-tools.md contract exactly. TestErrorMessages class verifies 8 error message formats: Invalid JSON, Missing required field 'messages', Missing required field 'metadata', Invalid metadata.layer value (with value), messages[0].role must be 'system', reasoning/direct think block mismatches, metadata field value not in valid values.

completion_promises.criterion_text: JSON content with embedded newlines/escapes written as single valid JSON line
completion_promises.evidence: json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False) serializes content with embedded newlines as escaped \n characters, producing a single line. TestEmbeddedNewlines verifies content with newlines produces exactly 1 line in the output file, and parsed content preserves the original newlines.
No truncation logic exists in the implementation. TestLargeContent writes a 120KB example and verifies the full content is present in the parsed output file.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Partial write failure leaves file in consistent state
completion_promises.evidence: Each write is an atomic append (single f.write call + flush). OSError is caught and returns error string. Previous successful lines remain intact since append mode doesn't modify existing content.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All errors returned as descriptive strings, never raised as exceptions (D7)
completion_promises.evidence: Every error path returns a string starting with 'Error:'. JSON parse errors caught with try/except. I/O errors caught with try/except OSError. TestD7NoExceptions verifies invalid JSON, missing fields, and I/O errors all return strings (isinstance check) without raising.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Both files compile cleanly (py_compile). Code follows PEP 8, uses type hints, has docstrings, and follows the project's code style (from __future__ import annotations, pathlib.Path, logging module).
